actual_coverage.reasoning: Predicted question bridges multi-year EPS/ROIC drivers (yield, costs, capital allocation), eliciting a similar discussion of durable multi-year growth vs pre-pandemic.
Predicted addresses booking/visibility and Med rebound, but not the specific Q4 yield acceleration/confidence needed to hit full-year targets.
Directly targets Med moderation magnitude, booking curve shape, and downside/guidance sensitivity.
Asks for peak-to-trough Med impact and current booking run-rate vs pre-conflict, aligning with 'turning the corner' and timing beyond Q3.
Quantifies APD uplift and ramp/payback for private destinations including Perfect Day Mexico; close but lacks Galveston penetration detail.
Predicted addresses Mexico timing/mitigation, but not the specific construction pause/resumption confirmation.
Predicted asks about conservatism; actual asks if 2024 Europe shortfall fully reverses in 2027. Partial theme of outlook but different ask.
Directly splits yield between like-for-like vs new hardware/private destinations and aligns with the regional (Europe) growth question.
Actual focuses on itinerary changes and deferrals due to higher fuel; predicted focuses on fuel hedge/sensitivity rather than deployment or deferral choices.
Predicted discusses hedge sizing/philosophy in a high fuel environment, substantially overlapping the first part of the actual; does not cover the NCC ex-fuel vs capacity threshold.
Directly connects loyalty/card changes to booking frequency/APD and yield, matching implications for net yield growth from higher repeat penetration.
Predicted mentions air capacity/connectivity mitigation for Med/Mex; actual asks about NA customers’ behavior under higher NA airfares.

missed_actual_themes: Quarterly yield cadence and confidence in Q4 acceleration to achieve full-year yield guide
Status of construction/resumption at Perfect Day Mexico following environmental pause
Whether 2024 European yield headwinds should fully reverse in 2027
Operational itinerary changes or initiative deferrals due to higher fuel costs (vs harvesting efficiencies)
Impact of higher North American airfares on NA customer behavior and booking patterns
overpredicted_themes: Capital allocation, buybacks, leverage targets and path to investment grade; financing mix (unsecured vs ECA) and covenants
Granular fuel EPS sensitivity tables beyond hedge approach specifics
Detailed JV/TUI contribution reconciliations and guidance bridges
West Coast Mexico demand softness/yield impact and mitigation
Highly granular APD premiums/payback for specific new ships and private destinations
Quarterly beat decomposition and explicit guidance conservatism buffers
summary: Coverage was solid on Mediterranean booking recovery, yield decomposition (hardware vs like-for-like), hedging strategy, and loyalty/card monetization, driving 7/12 actuals covered (avg score 2.75). Precision was moderate (62/110 useful; avg score 2.29), with many overpredictions on capital allocation/financing and JV details. Key misses were Q4 yield cadence confidence, Mexico construction status, 2027 Europe recapture, fuel-driven itinerary changes/deferrals, and NA airfare effects.
